#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);
    
    // Get the singleton observer graph instance
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship: observer watches target
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);
    
    // Trigger notification by changing the target
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Return notification status and call count
    return {observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestObserverClosure : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Create source and observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>("source");
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName);

    // Get the singleton observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Add observer relationship: source observes the observer
    graph.addObserver(source, observer);

    // Notify the source - should trigger the observer
    source->valueChanged();
    
    // Verify observer was notified
    if (!observer->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    // Reset observer state
    observer->valueChanged(false);

    // Close the observer node
    observer->close();

    // Notify source again
    source->valueChanged();

    // Verify observer was not notified after closure
    if (observer->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestMultipleObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestMultipleObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    
    // Create observer nodes
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        observers.push_back(std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name));
    }
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add all observers to watch target
    for (const auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }
    
    // Notify target
    target->notify();
    
    // Count notifications and verify all observers were notified
    int totalCallCount = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    
    for (const auto& observer : observers) {
        totalCallCount += observer->getCallCount();
        if (observer->getCallCount() == 0) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }
    
    return {totalCallCount, allNotified};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age) : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name.get(); }
    int getAge() const { return m_age.get(); }

    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name = name; }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age = age; }

private:
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    PersonRecord person(initialName, initialAge);
    
    // Create a reactive calculation that depends on both name and age
    auto name_dep = person.m_name;
    auto age_dep = person.m_age;
    auto info = reaction::calc(name_dep, age_dep, [](const std::string& n, int a) {
        return "Name: " + n + ", Age: " + std::to_string(a);
    });
    
    // Update the fields
    person.setName(newName);
    person.setAge(newAge);
    
    return info.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto a = reaction::var(initialA);
    auto b = reaction::var(initialB);
    auto recompute_count = reaction::var(0);
    
    // Create calculation that tracks recomputation count
    auto sum_calc = reaction::calc([a, b, &recompute_count]() {
        int result = a.get() + b.get();
        recompute_count.set(recompute_count.get() + 1);
        return result;
    });
    
    // Perform non-batch updates
    a.set(nonBatchA);
    b.set(nonBatchB);
    int non_batch_count = recompute_count.get();
    
    // Execute batch updates
    reaction::batch_execute([&]() {
        a.set(batchA);
        b.set(batchB);
    });
    
    int batch_count = recompute_count.get();
    
    return std::make_tuple(sum_calc.get(), non_batch_count, batch_count);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {

}

}  // namespace test_reaction

1. Create reactive variable
2. Create variables to track recomputation count and last processed value
3. Create filter-triggered calculation
4. Apply filter
5. Update sensor with first value
6. Record recompute count after first update
7. Update sensor with second value
8. Record recompute count after second update
9. Get final status
10. Return final status and counts

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>
 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

auto calc = reaction::calc([&]() {

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>
#include <cmath>
#include <limits>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    auto sensor = reaction::var(initialValue);
    auto filtered_sensor = reaction::filter(reaction::ChangeFilter(diffThreshold), sensor);
    auto count = reaction::var(0);
    auto last_filtered_value = reaction::var(std::numeric_limits<double>::quiet_NaN());
    
    auto status_calc = reaction::calc([filtered_sensor, last_filtered_value, count, levelThreshold]() {
        double current = filtered_sensor.get();
        if (std::isnan(last_filtered_value.get())) {
            last_filtered_value.set(current);
            count.set(1);
        } else {
            last_filtered_value.set(current);
            count.set(count.get() + 1);
        }
        return current > levelThreshold ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";
    });
    
    auto status = reaction::var("NORMAL");
    status = status_calc;
    
    // Update sensor with first value
    sensor.set(firstUpdate);
    int count_after_first = count.get();
    
    // Update with second value
    sensor.set(secondUpdate);
    int count_after_second = count.get();
    
    return {status.get(), count_after_first, count_after_second};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct CreateFactoryResult {
    int initialExpressionValue;
    int updatedExpressionValue;
    int squaredValueAfterUpdate;
};

CreateFactoryResult test_reactive_create_factory(int baseValue, int increment) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto var = reaction::create(baseValue);
    
    // Create reactive expression: var + increment
    auto expr = reaction::create([var]() { return var->get() + increment; });
    
    // Create reactive calculation: var squared
    auto calc = reaction::create([var]() { return var->get() * var->get(); });
    
    // Get initial values
    int initialExprValue = expr->get();
    
    // Update the reactive variable
    var->set(baseValue + increment);
    
    // Get updated values
    int updatedExprValue = expr->get();
    int squaredValueAfterUpdate = calc->get();
    
    return CreateFactoryResult{
        initialExprValue,
        updatedExprValue,
        squaredValueAfterUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    int finalValue = calculation.get();

        recomputeCountAfterBatch,
        finalValue
    };
}

    nodeB.close();
    nodeC.close();

}

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class CacheProbeNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        reaction::ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }
};

struct CacheStatsSnapshot {
    size_t graphEntriesBeforeCleanup;
    size_t graphEntriesAfterCleanup;
    double graphHitRatioAfterCleanup;
};

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    CacheProbeNode nodeA, nodeB, nodeC;
    
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    graph.registerNode(&nodeA);
    graph.registerNode(&nodeB);
    graph.registerNode(&nodeC);
    
    nodeA.addObserver(&nodeB);
    nodeB.addObserver(&nodeC);
    
    // Create NodeSet (though usage isn't specified in problem details)
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    
    // Populate cache by triggering value changes
    nodeC.valueChanged(true);
    nodeB.valueChanged(true);
    
    auto statsBefore = graph.getStats();
    graph.cleanupOldEntries();
    auto statsAfter = graph.getStats();
    
    // Calculate hit ratio after cleanup
    double hitRatio = 0.0;
    if (statsAfter.hits + statsAfter.misses > 0) {
        hitRatio = static_cast<double>(statsAfter.hits) / (statsAfter.hits + statsAfter.misses);
    }
    
    nodeA.close();
    nodeB.close();
    nodeC.close();
    
    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        statsBefore.entries,
        statsAfter.entries,
        hitRatio
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyTransition {
    bool beforeEnable;
    bool afterEnable;
    bool afterReset;
    std::size_t threadCount;
};

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::get_thread_manager();
    
    threadManager.reset();
    threadManager.register_current_thread();
    std::size_t threadCount = threadManager.get_thread_count();
    
    bool beforeEnable = threadManager.is_thread_safety_enabled();
    threadManager.enable_thread_safety();
    bool afterEnable = threadManager.is_thread_safety_enabled();
    
    threadManager.reset();
    bool afterReset = threadManager.is_thread_safety_enabled();
    
    return ThreadSafetyTransition{
        .beforeEnable = beforeEnable,
        .afterEnable = afterEnable,
        .afterReset = afterReset,
        .threadCount = threadCount
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadGuardState {
    bool safetyBeforeGuard;
    bool safetyAfterGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard;
};

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();

    bool safetyBeforeGuard = tm.getThreadSafetyStatus();
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard = tm.getCurrentThreadCount();

    std::thread workerThread([&]() {
        reaction::ThreadRegistrationGuard guard;
        std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(10));
    });

    workerThread.join();

    bool safetyAfterGuard = tm.getThreadSafetyStatus();
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard = tm.getCurrentThreadCount();

    return ThreadGuardState{
        safetyBeforeGuard,
        safetyAfterGuard,
        threadCountBeforeGuard,
        threadCountAfterGuard
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <array>
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SmallPayload {
    int value;
    bool operator!=(const SmallPayload& other) const = default;
};

struct LargePayload {
    std::array<int, 64> data{};
    bool operator!=(const LargePayload& other) const = default;
};

struct SboStorageStatus {
    bool updateReportedChange;
    bool smallUsingSBO;
    std::size_t smallStackUsage;
    bool largeInitialized;
    bool largeUsingSBO;
};

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // Lambda to create large payload with consistent values
    auto makeLargePayload = [largeSeed](int seed) -> LargePayload {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (auto& element : payload.data) {
            element = seed;
        }
        return payload;
    };

    // Create SBOResource instances
    reaction::SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallResource(SmallPayload{initialSmall});
    reaction::SBOResource<LargePayload> largeResource(makeLargePayload(largeSeed));

    // Update small resource and capture change report
    bool updateReportedChange = smallResource.updateValue(SmallPayload{updatedSmall});

    // Retrieve storage information
    auto smallInfo = smallResource.getStorageInfo();
    auto largeInfo = largeResource.getStorageInfo();

    return SboStorageStatus{
        .updateReportedChange = updateReportedChange,
        .smallUsingSBO = smallInfo.isUsingSBO(),
        .smallStackUsage = smallInfo.stackUsage(),
        .largeInitialized = largeInfo.initialized(),
        .largeUsingSBO = largeInfo.isUsingSBO()
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/conditional_shared_mutex.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SharedMutexResult {
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety;
    bool trySharedWithSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety;
};

SharedMutexResult test_conditional_shared_mutex() {
    auto &threadManager = reaction::getThreadManager();
    threadManager.resetForTesting();

    reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex mutex;
    SharedMutexResult result{};

    // Test without thread safety
    threadManager.setThreadSafetyEnabled(false);
    
    // Attempt to acquire shared lock without safety
    result.trySharedWithoutSafety = mutex.trySharedLock();
    if (result.trySharedWithoutSafety) {
        mutex.unlockShared();
    }

    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLockGuard guard(mutex);
        result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = mutex.tryUniqueLock();
        if (result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety) {
            mutex.unlockUnique();
        }
    }

    // Test with thread safety enabled
    threadManager.setThreadSafetyEnabled(true);
    
    // Attempt to acquire shared lock with safety
    result.trySharedWithSafety = mutex.trySharedLock();
    if (result.trySharedWithSafety) {
        mutex.unlockShared();
    }

    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLockGuard guard(mutex);
        result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = mutex.tryUniqueLock();
        if (result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety) {
            mutex.unlockUnique();
        }
    }

    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

SboTraitSummary analyze_sbo_traits() {
    SboTraitSummary summary;

    summary.tinyForceHeap = tiny_traits.force_heap;
    summary.mediumConditionalSbo = medium_traits.conditional_sbo;
    summary.mediumForceHeap = medium_traits.force_heap;
    summary.largeForceHeap = large_traits.force_heap;
    
    // Set configuration thresholds
    summary.alwaysThreshold = config.always_threshold;
    summary.conditionalThreshold = config.conditional_threshold;
    
    // Calculate maximum stack usage (sum of sizes of SBO-eligible pods)
    std::size_t max_stack_usage = 0;
    if (tiny_traits.always_sbo || tiny_traits.conditional_sbo) {

    return summary;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    reaction::React<int> source(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create copy of source
    reaction::React<int> copy = source;
    
    // 3. Check validity before close
    bool firstValidBeforeClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    
    // 4. Check equality before close
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose = (copy == source);
    
    // 5. Close source node
    source.close();
    
    // 6. Check validity after close
    bool firstValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    bool copyValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(copy);
    
    // 7. Check equality after close with exception handling
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose = false;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    
    try {
        copiesEqualAfterClose = (copy == source);
    } catch (...) {
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }
    
    return ValiditySnapshot{
        firstValidBeforeClose,
        firstValidAfterClose,
        copiesEqualBeforeClose,
        copiesEqualAfterClose,
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    auto owner = std::make_unique<TrackingFieldNode>("owner");
    auto field = std::make_unique<TrackingFieldNode>("field");

    reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().addNode(owner.get());
    reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().addNode(field.get());

    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.registerNode(objectId, field.get());
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, "field", owner.get());

    field->valueChanged(true);
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete = (owner->notifications() > 0);
    owner->clear();

    fieldGraph.clearDependencies(owner.get());
    fieldGraph.removeObjectMappings(objectId);
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, "field", owner.get());

    field->valueChanged(true);
    bool notifiedAfterDelete = (owner->notifications() > 0);

    owner.reset();
    field.reset();

    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBeforeDelete, notifiedAfterDelete};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>
#include <chrono>
#include <optional>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

class IntCache : public CacheBase<int, int> {
public:
    using CacheBase<int, int>::insert;
    using CacheBase<int, int>::get;
    using CacheBase<int, int>::getStats;
    using CacheBase<int, int>::invalidateAll;
    using CacheBase<int, int>::triggerCleanup;
};

GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {

    auto initialStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesBefore = initialStats.currentSize;
    
    // Perform initial lookup (should be a miss)
    auto firstResult = cache.get(42);
    bool firstWasMiss = !firstResult.has_value();
    
    // Insert value into cache
    cache.insert(42, 100);
    
    // Perform second lookup (should be a hit)
    auto secondResult = cache.get(42);
    bool secondWasHit = secondResult.has_value();
    
    // Get cache statistics after insertion
    auto afterInsertStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert = afterInsertStats.currentSize;
    
    // Invalidate all cache entries and trigger cleanup
    cache.invalidateAll();
    cache.triggerCleanup();
    
    // Get cache statistics after invalidation
    auto afterInvalidationStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate = afterInvalidationStats.currentSize;
    
    // Calculate hit ratio
    std::size_t totalRequests = afterInsertStats.hitCount + afterInsertStats.missCount;
    double ratio = (totalRequests > 0) ? 
        static_cast<double>(afterInsertStats.hitCount) / totalRequests : 0.0;
    
    return GenericCacheStatsSnapshot{
        entriesBefore,
        entriesAfterInsert,
        entriesAfterInvalidate,
        afterInsertStats.hitCount,
        afterInsertStats.missCount,
        ratio,
        firstWasMiss,
        secondWasHit
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_thread_manager_force_register` function.

The function should:
1. Get thread manager instance
2. Reset thread manager state
3. Check thread safety before registration
4. Register main thread
5. Check thread safety after main thread registration
6. Create worker thread and register it
7. Wait for worker thread to complete
8. Check thread safety after worker thread registration
9. Return force register snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
